mnemonics: IDD the Data: Insert, Delete, Display. Remember it's like a party: Invite (Insert), De-invite (Delete), See who's there (Display).
keyPoints: Insertion requires finding the correct position to maintain data structure integrity.
Deletion must handle special cases like an empty structure or removing the first/last element.
Display (or traversal) visits each element without altering the structure's contents.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update pointers/references after an insertion or deletion, causing memory leaks or broken links.
Not checking if the structure is empty before attempting a deletion operation, leading to errors.
